s: The grammar pattern 〜な means 'Vru, (negative command - sentence ending particle)'
q: What does the grammar pattern '〜な' mean?
d: at the exact moment when something happens
then and only then will it occur
it is permitted and allowed to do so
you are required to complete this task
someone is supposed to act in this way
it is absolutely necessary to do it
as a direct result of what happened
consequently and as a logical outcome
e: Pattern: Verb dictionary form+な. Don't do that!

s: The grammar pattern 方が means '(often it'd be better to do __) Such-and-such an option is something'
q: What does the grammar pattern '方が' mean?
a: (often it'd be better to do __) Such-and-such an option is something
d: as it is, leave as it is, (remain) unchanged
following; in accordance with; as 〜, accordingly; in proportion to
and then, after that; Due to that
e: You'd better study then.

s: The grammar pattern より means 'than, rather than'
q: What does the grammar pattern 'より' mean?
d: this, especially (emphasis),very, emphasize the preceding word
of all the ... / especially / only / just
e: Pattern: Noun+より+..., Noun+は+Noun+より+.... Tokyo is larger than Kyoto.

s: The grammar pattern らしい means 'seem, look like, apparently, I heard'
q: What does the grammar pattern 'らしい' mean?
a: seem, look like, apparently, I heard
d: at the exact moment when something happens
then and only then will it occur
you are required to complete this task
it is absolutely necessary to do it
as a direct result of what happened
consequently and as a logical outcome
however and yet despite all this
e: Pattern: Noun+らしい, Verb (casual)+らしい, な-adjective (without な)+らしい, い-adjective+らしい. It seems Tanaka will resign

s: The grammar pattern にくい means 'Is difficult to do'
q: What does the grammar pattern 'にくい' mean?
e: He's difficult to understand, isn't he?

s: The grammar pattern ようになる means 'Indicates a change which has taken place or will take place.'
q: What does the grammar pattern 'ようになる' mean?
a: Indicates a change which has taken place or will take place.
d: whether, the question of (sub sentence question)
as it is, leave as it is, (remain) unchanged
e: I came to have an interest in Japan after I went to Japan

s: The grammar pattern 比べものにならない means 'there is no point comparing....'
q: What does the grammar pattern '比べものにならない' mean?
d: as it is, leave as it is, (remain) unchanged
just like, alike to, similar to
of all the ... / especially / only / just
e: When we talk about speed, there is no point comparing a plane and a ship.

s: The grammar pattern くらい、ぐらい (位) means 'to the extent that; so ~ that ~ almost ~; at least; the only ~; rather than ~'
q: What does the grammar pattern 'くらい、ぐらい (位)' mean?
a: to the extent that; so ~ that ~ almost ~; at least; the only ~; rather than ~
d: Talking about relative location of an object
such; this; like this; such.....as this; of this kind
Vte something has been purposely put into a state
of all the ... / especially / only / just
e: It's so warm this evening that we (almost) don't need a heater.

s: The grammar pattern のに means 'in order to, to'
q: What does the grammar pattern 'のに' mean?
e: It takes time to learn Japanese

s: The grammar pattern 〜ところ(だ) (所だ・処だ) means 'I am about to / I was about to'
q: What does the grammar pattern '〜ところ(だ) (所だ・処だ)' mean?
a: I am about to / I was about to
d: Vte something has been purposely put into a state
such; this; like this; such.....as this; of this kind
is, would be, probably is; isn't it?
Vte (Continuing states, experience, repetative events)/ doing currently
e: Pattern: V辞書形+ところ（だ）, Vている形+ところ（だ）, Vた形／Vていた形+ところ（だ）. He arrived just as I was about to give up.

s: The grammar pattern 〜は means ''ha (wa)' used to indicate contrast'
q: What does the grammar pattern '〜は' mean?
d: whether, the question of (sub sentence question)
Turn a phrase ending in a verb into a 'noun phrase'
In a flash, before one knows it, in a blink of an eye, in no time, flash by, (time) flies
e: I don't eat /meat/ (but I do eat fish).

s: The grammar pattern そう means 'I hear that'
q: What does the grammar pattern 'そう' mean?
d: to the extent that; so ~ that ~ almost ~; at least; the only ~; rather than ~
e: Pattern: Verb (casual)+そう+(だ), Noun+だ+そう+(だ), い-adjective+そう+(だ), な-adjective (without な)+だ+そう+(だ). I hear it's going to snow.

s: The grammar pattern 〜よ means '! (exclamation mark, add emphasis, denote new information being imparted)'
q: What does the grammar pattern '〜よ' mean?
a: ! (exclamation mark, add emphasis, denote new information being imparted)
d: time and place prepositions at in on
the same way as, as well as
e: The bus is coming!

s: The grammar pattern ように言う means 'V, To say to (do something)'
q: What does the grammar pattern 'ように言う' mean?
d: then and only then will it occur
you are required to complete this task
it is absolutely necessary to do it
as a direct result of what happened
consequently and as a logical outcome
however and yet despite all this
e: Pattern: Verb dictionary form+ように言う, Verb ~ない form+ように言う. My doctor told me to stop drinking.

s: The grammar pattern よう means 'appearance, looks, seems'
q: What does the grammar pattern 'よう' mean?
d: center on, in the middle of
I wonder.. (Based on か question particle.)
is, would be, probably is; isn't it?
e: Pattern: Verb (casual form)+よう, い-adjective+よう, な-adjective+な+よう, Noun+の+よう. A sound like rain falling

s: The grammar pattern ところで (所で・処で) means 'by the way'
q: What does the grammar pattern 'ところで (所で・処で)' mean?
e: by the way it looks like rain

s: The grammar pattern ~出す means 'begin/start...'
q: What does the grammar pattern '~出す' mean?
e: Pattern: Verb ます stem+出す. Rain started to fall.

d: It does not (necessarily) mean that 〜; I don't mean that 〜; It is not (true) that 〜; It is not the case that 〜
while doing A, I had a chance to do B, since doing A anyway, do B
e: Pattern: Verb (dictionary form)+の. Getting married is such an effort!

d: have just done something; have just finished doing something’
e: Won't it be great if we can go fishing tomorrow?

e: Pattern: Verb masu stem+なさい. Sit down.

d: Try to do something, but the nuance is that it was unsuccessful.
center on, in the middle of
Turn a phrase ending in a verb into a 'noun phrase'

e: Pattern: Phrase+反面+phrase. the reverse

it is absolutely necessary to do it
as a direct result of what happened
on the other hand from this perspective
e: I wound up laughing.

s: The grammar pattern 向き means 'for, facing, toward, aimed at'
q: What does the grammar pattern '向き' mean?
d: following; in accordance with; as 〜, accordingly; in proportion to
such; this; like this; such.....as this; of this kind
e: a PC for old people

s: The grammar pattern 貰う means 'to get'
q: What does the grammar pattern '貰う' mean?
e: my dad bought a camera for me

s: The grammar pattern と共に means 'as well as; with; along with; at the same time; when; as; while'
q: What does the grammar pattern 'と共に' mean?
a: as well as; with; along with; at the same time; when; as; while
d: In a flash, before one knows it, in a blink of an eye, in no time, flash by, (time) flies
toward; to; in contrast to; whereas; in regard to; in; per
must ____; really should _____; it won't do if you don't ____.
e: As I'm recovering from my illness, my appetite is coming back.

s: The grammar pattern うちに means 'while; during; as'
q: What does the grammar pattern 'うちに' mean?
e: みるみるうちに太った。/ He gained weight just while I was watching him.

s: The grammar pattern ところに、ところへ、ところを means '(Just) when'
q: What does the grammar pattern 'ところに、ところへ、ところを' mean?
e: Pattern: Verb（ている形）+ところ, Verb（た形）+ところ, Noun+の+ところ, い-adjective+ところ. Just when I had decided to call her, she phoned me.

s: The grammar pattern 意向形と思う means 'I think I will (do)'
q: What does the grammar pattern '意向形と思う' mean?
d: time and place prepositions at in on
It does not (necessarily) mean that 〜; I don't mean that 〜; It is not (true) that 〜; It is not the case that 〜
e: 今から銀行へ行こうと思います。/ I think I will go to the bank right now.

s: The grammar pattern 儚い means 'hopeless, fleeting, transient, fickle'
q: What does the grammar pattern '儚い' mean?
d: whether, the question of (sub sentence question)
of all the ... / especially / only / just
seem, look like, apparently, I heard
e: Unrequited (hopeless) love

s: The grammar pattern 積もりだった means 'I had the intention to do (but I didn't)'
q: What does the grammar pattern '積もりだった' mean?
a: I had the intention to do (but I didn't)
d: I am about to / I was about to
crossing without meeting (e.g. letters in the post, people on the road)
e: 僕は彼女と結婚するつもりだった。/ I had the intention to marry her (but I didn't).

s: The grammar pattern 〜としても means 'even if; assuming'
q: What does the grammar pattern '〜としても' mean?
e: Pattern: Verb casual form+としても, い-adjective+としても, な-adjective (without な)+だ+としても, Noun+だ+としても. Even if it was true, I wouldn't believe it of him!

s: The grammar pattern やすい means 'Easy to V'
q: What does the grammar pattern 'やすい' mean?
e: Pattern: Verb ます stem+やすい. A beautiful woman is easy to fall in love with / 美女は惚れやすい。

s: The grammar pattern 続ける means 'keep on doing ...'
q: What does the grammar pattern '続ける' mean?
e: Pattern: ます stem+つづける. keep on eating (sweet stuff)

s: The grammar pattern って means '...said; no matter what'
q: What does the grammar pattern 'って' mean?
e: He said that.....

s: The grammar pattern あまり means 'not really'
q: What does the grammar pattern 'あまり' mean?
e: 彼女はあまりきれいじゃない。　She isn't very pretty.

s: The grammar pattern によって means 'depending on'
q: What does the grammar pattern 'によって' mean?
e: Pattern: Noun+によって. depending on the weather, I will come

s: The grammar pattern どうせ means 'after all; eventually; at best'
q: What does the grammar pattern 'どうせ' mean?
d: consequently and as a logical outcome
however and yet despite all this
to repeat the same action over
to attempt something again and again
after a long time has passed
e: After all, there's nothing permanent.

s: The grammar pattern であろう means 'I guess, wonder, probably, isn't it?'
q: What does the grammar pattern 'であろう' mean?
a: I guess, wonder, probably, isn't it?
d: In order to, because of, for
I wonder if...　（A type of question mark)
e: I guess he will never come back the place.

s: The grammar pattern になる means 'To become'
q: What does the grammar pattern 'になる' mean?
e: It's become a real mess!

s: The grammar pattern 〜わ means '(adds emphasis) [female]'
q: What does the grammar pattern '〜わ' mean?
e: That's just fine.

s: The grammar pattern 切る means 'verb-totally'
q: What does the grammar pattern '切る' mean?
e: totally sold out

s: The grammar pattern について means 'regarding, about'
q: What does the grammar pattern 'について' mean?
e: lets talk about the project

s: The grammar pattern 前に means 'before [action]'
q: What does the grammar pattern '前に' mean?
e: Pattern: Verb dictionary form+まえに, Noun+の+まえに. before going to the UK

s: The grammar pattern くらい means 'about, as'
q: What does the grammar pattern 'くらい' mean?
e: Pattern: Noun+くらい/ぐらい. (about) as normal as any other guy on the street.

s: The grammar pattern 下さい means 'please'
q: What does the grammar pattern '下さい' mean?
e: Pattern: て-form+ください, を+ください. Please give me that

s: The grammar pattern ばかり means 'just (just recently) / nothing but'
q: What does the grammar pattern 'ばかり' mean?
d: to seems ~ (of somebody else's emotions & desires), to show sign of ~, to appear ~
e: Pattern: Verb casual past た-form+ばかり, Verb て-form+ばかり, Verb dictionary form+ばかり, Noun+ばかり. I just bought it / you do nothing but play around
